REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHU-HSIN YANG'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 7, 2013 PER THE EX PARTE ORDER FILED ON FEBRUARY 11, 2013. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CHU-HSIN YANG'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION REGARDING UNILATERAL ACTIONS BY PATRIZ REGALADO IN OPERATION OF CORE STUDIO SPX, LLC IS DENIED. MS. YANG HAS FAILED TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS OR IRREPARABLE HARM. THE EVIDENCE TENDS TO SHOW THAT MS. REGALADO HAS ACTED WITHIN HER AUTHORITY UNDER THE OPERATING AGREEMENT AND IN THE INTERESTS OF THE LLC. IN ANY EVENT, ANY HARM CAN BE REMEDIED BY MONEY DAMAGES. FURTHER, AS ALLEGED IN HER CROSS-COMPLAINT, MS. YANG MAY PURSUE AN ELECTION TO PURCHASE MEMBERSHIP INTEREST PURSUANT TO CORP. CODE 17351(B). MS. YANG HAS NOT MET HER BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NECESSARY IN THE INTERIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHU-HSIN YANG'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF PATRIZ REGALADO'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHU-HSIN YANG TO POST SECURITY - MOTION FOR SECURITY GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,000 TO BE POSTED BY DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHU-HSIN YANG. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT CONCEDES THAT AT ITS CORE THIS CASE IS A TWO PERSON DISPUTE OVER DISSOLUTION OF THE LLC. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE THAT THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE PROSECUTION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT WILL BENEFIT THE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY. BOND TO BE POSTED 10 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. REQUEST FOR THE IMPOSITION OF SANCTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077 = (302/MJM)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-07-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RONY SAGY, TEL. NO. 986-0900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK REED, TEL. NO. 477-3934 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHU-HSIN YANG'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction regarding Unilateral Actions by Patriz Regalado in Operation of Core Studio SPX, LLC is Denied. Ms. Yang has failed to show a likelihood of success on the merits or irreparable harm. The evidence tends to show that Ms. Regalado has acted within her authority under the Operating Agreement and in the interests of the LLC. In any event, any harm can be remedied by money damages. Further, as alleged in her Cross-Complaint, Ms. Yang may pursue an election to purchase membership interest pursuant to Corp. Code 17351(b). Ms. Yang has not met her burden of showing that a preliminary injunction is necessary in the interim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-13-2013 Continued To Apr-10-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Continued To Mar-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHU-HSIN YANG'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is continued to March 7, 2013 per the ex parte order filed on February 11, 2013. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Deft And X-Complainant To Post Security
Off Calendar Jan-09-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RONY SAGY, TEL. NO. 986-0900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRIZ REGALADO's Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant And Cross-Complainant Chu-Hsin Yang To Post Security - Motion for security granted in the amount of $10,000 to be posted by Defendant/Cross-Complainant Chu-Hsin Yang. Defendant/Cross-Complainant concedes that at its core this case is a two person dispute over dissolution of the LLC. Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant has shown by a preponderance of the evidence that there is no reasonable possibility that the prosecution of the cross-complaint will benefit the limited liability company. Bond to be posted 10 days after notice of entry of order. Request for the imposition of sanction is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Deft And X-Complainant To Post Security

PLAINTIFF PATRIZ REGALADO's Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant And Cross-Complainant To Post Security is continued to January 9, 2013 per request of Defendant's counsel. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 = (302/MJM)

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF PATRIZ REGALADO's Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant And cross-Complainant To Post Security is continued to January 3, 2013, on the Court's motion. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Deft And X-Complainant To Post Security
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Continued To Dec-26-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rony Sagy, Esq. of Sagy Law Associates, Tel. 415.986.0900, for plaintiff/cross defendant;
Mark Rice, Esq. of McNeil Silveira Rice & Wiley, Tel. 415.472.3434, for defendant/cross complainant.

The matter was discussed. The Court was presented with a copy of the stipulation and order staying action against defendant/cross-defendant Core Studio SPX LLC only. Therefore, the court vacated its tentative ruling and continued PLAINTIFF PATRIZ REGALADO's Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant And Cross-Complainant Chu Hsin-Yang To Post Security to December 26, 2012 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976.
